seoTitle: How to Redirect a Domain in Cloudflare to Another Site
metaDescription: Forward one domain to another website using Cloudflare's Redirect Rules: set up a proxied placeholder record and write the redirect expression.
intro: Forwarding an entire domain to a different website in Cloudflare requires a proxied DNS placeholder record before any redirect rule can take effect, which is the step most people miss. This guide walks through setting up that record and configuring a Redirect Rule to send visitors to your target destination with the right status code.

steps.heading: Open your domain's DNS panel
steps.body: Log into Cloudflare and go to the DNS records section for the domain you want to redirect.

steps.heading: Create a proxied placeholder record
steps.body: Add an A or AAAA record and make sure it's proxied (orange cloud) — Redirect Rules only process traffic that passes through Cloudflare's edge.

steps.heading: Go to Redirect Rules
steps.body: Navigate to the Rules section in the sidebar and select Redirect Rules.

steps.heading: Create and name your rule
steps.body: Click Create Rule and give it a descriptive name so you can identify it later.

steps.heading: Match the incoming hostname
steps.body: Use the expression builder to match requests to your specific source hostname accurately.

steps.heading: Set the destination and status code
steps.body: Enter your target destination URL, choose a 301 Permanent Redirect status, then click Deploy to activate the forwarding.

faq.question: Why do I need a DNS record if I'm just redirecting the domain?
faq.answer: Cloudflare's Redirect Rules only trigger on traffic that reaches its edge network, which requires a proxied DNS record to exist even if it just points to a placeholder.

faq.question: Should I use a 301 or 302 status code for a domain redirect?
faq.answer: Use 301 for a permanent move — it tells search engines and browsers to update their records to the new destination.

faq.question: Can I redirect a subdomain instead of the whole domain?
faq.answer: Yes, you can scope the expression to match a specific subdomain hostname instead of the whole apex domain.

faq.question: How fast does a Redirect Rule take effect after deploying?
faq.answer: Changes typically propagate across Cloudflare's edge network within seconds to a couple of minutes.
